title: Study finds Chinese and Russian code in US military apps
summary: Researchers from Purdue University found that nearly two-thirds of 220+ apps marketed to US military personnel contain third-party code from China, Russia, and other nations, including the Huawei SDK. This poses a national security risk as third-party code can be remotely updated to spyware, potentially compromising sensitive military personnel data and locations. The Huawei SDK was found in at least one case smuggled as a dependency without the developer&\#x27;s knowledge. While no data transfer to Huawei servers was observed, the code can be activated remotely.
context: Mobile apps often include third-party SDKs for functions like analytics. However, SDKs from adversarial nations can introduce supply chain risks. The US Department of Defense has previously reported surveillance via commercial location data.

references.title: Apps Marketed to US Troops Are Shipping Chinese and Russian Code | WIRED
